results.title: Randstad Digital Acquires Torc, the AI-powered Digital Talent Marketplace - HR Observer
results.author: Editorial Bureau
results.text:
Randstad Digital Acquires Torc, the AI-powered Digital Talent Marketplace - HR Observer

(L to R) - Michael Morris, CEO & Co-Founder of Torc, and Francisco D'Souza, Co-Founder and Managing Partner of Recognize

Mumbai, May 17, 2024: Randstad NV, the world’s leading talent company announces that Randstad Digital has acquired Torc – a next-generation AI-powered talent marketplace platform with more than 25,000 digital talent enrolled worldwide, with a specific current emphasis on LATAM, the US & India. Torc, with its AI-driven platform, enables skill-based matching to connect digital talent to clients globally. This acquisition allows Randstad Digital, a leading digital enablement company, to further accelerate clients’ access to digital talent, including global delivery centers, to meet its digital ambitions across cloud, data, digital engineering, and customer experience capabilities.

“The Torc acquisition strengthens Randstad’s partner for talent strategy in our Digital specialization. Digital transformation needs access to specialized talent at scale and speed globally. Torc, with its enterprise-grade AI-powered talent marketplace, will redefine the talent experience, and speed of delivery, and strengthen Randstad Digital’s capabilities in enabling our client’s digital transformation,” said Sander van’ t Noordende, CEO of Randstad.

“As enterprises redefine the digital business model, the demand for specialized skills in AI, Cloud, Data, and CX, continues to increase,” said Venu Lambu, CEO of Randstad Digital. “The acquisition of Torc is a direct response to this evolution and client needs. It expands our access to a diverse range of talent skill sets and a robust developer community from assessment to productivity. We are extremely excited to welcome an industry thought leader in open talent platforms, Michael Morris, Torc CEO and Co-Founder, and his team to Randstad Digital.”

“Recognize is proud to have incubated Torc alongside a management team who are visionaries at the forefront of open talent platform innovation. As part of Randstad Digital, Torc will further accelerate the adoption of AI-enabled talent experience and delivery. Together, they will define the next-generation delivery model for technology and digital skills and services,” said Francisco D’Souza, Co-Founder and Managing Partner of Recognize.

### About The Author

#### Editorial Bureau

HR Observer is a leading digital platform delivering human-resources news, insights, and industry intelligence. Built for HR leaders, CXOs, and C-suite decision-makers, HR Observer offers timely and authoritative coverage on recruitment and talent trends, leadership changes, workforce dynamics, organisational strategy, employer branding, and the rapidly evolving future of work. For enquiries, contact us at editorial@hrobserver.com

See author's posts

- Executive Movement

The Next Chapter in Global Talent Sourcing: Torc and Randstad Digital Unite - Torc window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || []; function gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments);} gtag('js', new Date()); gtag('config', 'G-2ECSE2JK1M'); window.datalayer = window.datalayer || []; function gtag() {datalayer.push (arguments) ;} gtag ('js', new Date ()); gtag ('config', 'AW-11255171080'); --

# The Next Chapter in Global Talent Sourcing: Torc and Randstad Digital Unite

May 1, 2025

I am thrilled to announce that Torc, the AI-powered next-gen global talent platform, has been acquired by Randstad Digital, the world’s leading provider of talent and digital enablement services. Joining Randstad Digital isn't just a new chapter for Torc employees, customers and our coveted developer community—it's a transformational leap in the global talent sourcing industry that promises unmatched innovation, speed and scalability.‍Strategic Synergy‍

Torc’s vision has always been bold and ambitious. We’ve maintained an unwavering loyalty to the physics of work, career development and providing the ultimate enterprise talent matching experience. Our goal was to advance how software developers gain access to incredible jobs and how companies find skilled technology talent.

Torc’s vision will only accelerate as part of Randstad Digital.

We’ve been working closely for the past six months and quickly realized our synergies. Together through experience, progressive technology, and digital enablement, Randstad Digital and Torc will forever redefine how companies and talent engage.

‍A Foundation Built on Experience‍

Torc’s journey began with a seasoned team of open talent industry pioneers advocating relentlessly for the value of digital staffing models. Torc Co-founder Dave Messinger and I, along with our COO Mike Cardillo, were previously with Topcoder, the crowdsourcing leader. Torc’s Chairman Frank D’Souza, Founder and long-time CEO of Cognizant; Board members like Rob Biederman of Asymmetric Capital Partners, as well as Torc CRO Pat Griffin, both previously of Catalant; and Torc’s Head of Talent Operations Mark Bosma, previously from Toptal and Marketerhire, have all have brought valuable expertise in scaling businesses, digital platforms, and global talent communities. In two and a half years, our collective experience helped build and grow an industry-leading company that disrupted the talent marketplace industry for the better. This team was essential in realizing the untapped potential of digital platforms in mainstream talent sourcing, a transition that has been in progress for more than two decades and accelerated by the global shift in work dynamics during the pandemic.‍Expanding Torc’s Reach‍

Torc has established a robust global network anchored in an AI-powered talent platform, cloud-native technology community, and enterprise customer base. In the past 18 months we’ve had a significant focus on LATAM, driven largely by high demand for nearshore talent from U.S.-based

results.text:
Randstad Digital Acquires Torc, the AI-powered Digital Talent Marketplace

# Randstad Digital Acquires Torc, the AI-powered Digital Talent Marketplace

Talent-acquisition News Talent management

#### Acquisition strengthens digital enablement capabilities with access to 25,000+ digital talent.

Randstad NV, the world’s leading talent company announces that Randstad Digital has acquired Torc – a next-generation AI-powered talent marketplace platform with more than 25,000 digital talent enrolled worldwide, with a specific current emphasis on LATAM, the US & India. Torc, with its AI-driven platform, enables skill-based matching to connect digital talent to clients globally. This acquisition allows Randstad Digital, a leading digital enablement company, to further accelerate clients’ access to digital talent, including global delivery centers, to meet its digital ambitions across cloud, data, digital engineering, and customer experience capabilities.

“The Torc acquisition strengthens Randstad’s partner for talent strategy in our Digital specialization. Digital transformation needs access to specialized talent at scale and speed globally. Torc, with its enterprise-grade AI-powered talent marketplace, will redefine the talent experience, and speed of delivery, and strengthen Randstad Digital’s capabilities in enabling our client’s digital transformation,” said Sander van‘ t Noordende, CEO of Randstad.

“As enterprises redefine the digital business model, the demand for specialized skills in AI, Cloud, Data, and CX, continues to increase,” said Venu Lambu, CEO of Randstad Digital. “The acquisition of Torc is a direct response to this evolution and client needs. It expands our access to a diverse range of talent skill sets and a robust developer community from assessment to productivity. We are extremely excited to welcome an industry thought leader in open talent platforms, Michael Morris, Torc CEO and Co-Founder, and his team to Randstad Digital.”

Latest Hrtech Insights: Welcome to the Age of Work/Life Empowerment

“Recognize is proud to have incubated Torc alongside a management team who are visionaries at the forefront of open talent platform innovation. As part of Randstad Digital, Torc will further accelerate the adoption of AI-enabled talent experience and delivery. Together, they will define the next-generation delivery model for technology and digital skills and services,” said Francisco D’Souza, Co-Founder and Managing Partner of Recognize.

Randstad Digital is a trusted digital enablement partner that facilitates accelerated transformation for businesses by providing global talent, capacity, and solutions across specialized domains. Our digital talent solutions allow you to seamlessly scale your team while connecting you with skilled professionals around the world who align with your chosen technologies. Our focus lies in managed programs, and we empower businesses to move at speed

DIEMAN, Netherlands, May 16, 2024 /PRNewswire/ — Today, Randstad NV, the world’s leading talent company announces that Randstad Digital has acquired Torc – a next-generation AI-powered talent marketplace platform with more than 25,000 digital talent enrolled worldwide, with a specific current emphasis on LATAM, the US & India. Torc, with its AI-driven platform, enables skill-based matching to connect digital talent to clients globally. This acquisition allows Randstad Digital, a leading digital enablement company, to further accelerate clients’ access to digital talent, including global delivery centers, to meet its digital ambitions across cloud, data, digital engineering, and customer experience capabilities.

“The Torc acquisition strengthens Randstad’s partner for talent strategy in our Digital specialization. Digital transformation needs access to specialized talent at scale and speed globally. Torc, with its enterprise-grade AI-powered talent marketplace, will redefine the talent experience, and speed of delivery, and strengthen Randstad Digital’s capabilities in enabling our client’s digital transformation,” said Sander van’ t Noordende, CEO of Randstad.

“As enterprises redefine the digital business model, the demand for specialized skills in AI, Cloud, Data, and CX, continues to increase,” said Venu Lambu, CEO of Randstad Digital. “The acquisition of Torc is a direct response to this evolution and client needs. It expands our access to a diverse range of talent skill sets and a robust developer community from assessment to productivity. We are extremely excited to welcome an industry thought leader in open talent platforms, Michael Morris, Torc CEO and Co-Founder, and his team to Randstad Digital.”

“Recognize is proud to have incubated Torc alongside a management team who are visionaries at the forefront of open talent platform innovation. As part of Randstad Digital, Torc will further accelerate the adoption of AI-enabled talent experience and delivery. Together, they will define the next-generation delivery model for technology and digital skills and services,” said Francisco D’Souza, Co-Founder and Managing Partner of Recognize.
